settings.intro: Used to personalise the app and to estimate starting weights for bodyweight-ratio exercises (e.g. bench press).
settings.name: Name
settings.hintBodyweight: Leave empty to use the default estimate (90 kg).

builder.startTitle: How do you want to start?
builder.startSubtitle: Pick a saved routine or build today's workout from muscle groups.
builder.noRoutinesHint: No routines saved yet — tap "Create Routine" on the home screen to save one.
builder.muscleSubtitleRoutine: Tap one or more for this routine.
builder.muscleSubtitleSession: Tap one or more to train today.
builder.exercisesSubtitleSession: Pick exercises for each muscle group.
builder.configureSubtitleRoutine: Pick a sets×reps scheme and starting weight for each exercise.
builder.configureSubtitleLoaded: Loaded routine "{{name}}". Tweak anything before starting.
builder.configureSubtitleSession: Pick a sets×reps scheme and confirm starting weight.
builder.routinePlaceholder: e.g. Push day, Pull A, Legs heavy

active.empty: No exercises yet. Tap "Add exercise" below.
active.discardMessage: Throw away this session? This cannot be undone.
active.saveRoutineSub: Reuse this session next time — name it to save, or skip to just finish.
active.saveRoutinePlaceholder: e.g. Push day, Pull A, Legs heavy

dashboard.noStatsSub: Complete a training session to start building your dashboard.
dashboard.noWow: Train another week to see week-over-week changes.
dashboard.deloadBanner: ⚠ Deload week detected — volume {{pct}} vs prior 4-week average.
dashboard.infoSummaryBody: Recap of the current calendar week (Mon–Sun): how many sessions you completed, total kg moved, week-over-week change per muscle group, and any new PRs hit. The card auto-expands on Mondays so you can review last week at a glance.
dashboard.infoOneRmBody:
One-Rep Max — the heaviest weight you could lift for a single rep, estimated from your normal working sets using the Epley formula:

  weight × (1 + reps / 30)

The tile shows the all-time best estimate for whichever of Bench Press, Back Squat, Deadlift, or Overhead Press you have logged most often (auto-picked). Warmup sets are excluded.
dashboard.infoVolumeBody:
Total kg moved this week (sets × reps × weight, summed across every working set in every session). The primary hypertrophy indicator: the more weight your muscles displaced, the more growth stimulus they got.

Warmups and undone sets are not counted.
dashboard.infoSessionsBody: Number of training sessions you completed since Monday. A session counts the moment you tap "Complete training", regardless of how many exercises were in it.
dashboard.infoPrsBody:
Personal Records hit since Monday, across three axes per exercise:

• Weight PR — heaviest single set ever for that lift
• Reps PR — most reps you've ever done at that weight or heavier
• Volume PR — biggest single-set volume (reps × weight) ever

PRs are detected when you complete a session and saved permanently. The set row in the active workout shows a gold badge as soon as you log a set that would beat history.
dashboard.infoSetsBody:
Count of completed working sets for each muscle category this week. A common rule of thumb is 10–20 hard sets per muscle per week for hypertrophy — use this to balance your week and avoid over- or under-training a group.

Legs combines quads, hamstrings, glutes and calves. Arms combines biceps and triceps. Abs are tracked separately and not shown here.
dashboard.infoLastTrainedBody: How many days since each muscle category last appeared in a completed session. Anything over 5 days gets a ⚠ flag — that muscle is likely losing the previous training stimulus.
dashboard.infoDeloadBody: A passive flag shown when this week's total volume is below 60% of the average of the previous 4 weeks. Could be intentional (planned recovery week) or accidental (life got in the way). No notification — just an FYI.

history.empty: No sessions yet. Complete a training to see it here.

exerciseList.empty: No exercises logged yet. Complete a session to start tracking progression per exercise.

exerciseDetail.infoPrBody:
The most recent Personal Record set for this exercise, across any of three axes:

• Weight PR — heaviest single set ever
• Reps PR — most reps you've ever done at this weight or heavier
• Volume PR — biggest single-set volume (reps × weight) ever

Warmup sets are excluded. The card shows whichever PR was hit most recently; the full timeline of every PR for this exercise lives in the "Recent PRs" list further down.
exerciseDetail.infoOverloadBody:
How fast your top working-set weight is climbing on this exercise, measured as a least-squares linear regression over the last 4 weeks of completed sessions.

A positive slope (e.g. +1.5 kg/week) means you are progressively overloading — the prerequisite for hypertrophy. A flat or negative slope means it is time to push the weight, change the rep range, or check recovery.

Needs at least 3 data points in the window. Updates automatically as you log more sessions.
exerciseDetail.infoRangesBody: Filters the two charts below to the last 1 month, 3 months, or all-time. Useful for spotting a recent trend without years of history compressing the lines.
exerciseDetail.infoTopWeightBody: Heaviest weight you used in a single working set during each session, plotted over time. Best raw indicator of strength gain on a given exercise. Stagnation here for several weeks signals the lift has stalled.
exerciseDetail.infoEst1RmBody:
Per-session estimated one-rep max for this exercise, computed with the Epley formula on your heaviest working set:

  weight × (1 + reps / 30)

More stable than the top-set chart because it normalises across rep ranges (a 5×5 day and a 3×10 day become comparable). The slow upward drift here is the signal you want to see.
exerciseDetail.infoRecentPrsBody: Every PR ever achieved on this exercise, newest first. Shown as the type (Weight / Reps / Volume), the achievement, and the date. Each PR was detected automatically when you completed a session.
